items.title: Trumpâ€™s sweeping tariffs send shockwaves across financial markets | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Financial markets around the world have taken a beating for a second day, as investors fear US President Donald Trump's trade tariffs could trigger a global recession.
New York's Dow Jones share index plunged 5-and-a-half percent on Friday â€“ more than two thousand points â€“ as Wall Street posted its steepest losses since the pandemic. European and Asian shares also tumbled.

items.title: Trump 2.0 and Taiwan: Will Trump cause or prevent war? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Trumpâ€™s new term has already brought turmoil to geopolitics and the global economy, but few places are watching events with more caution than Taiwan, the self-ruled democracy claimed by Beijing. Implicit US military support for the island has helped preserve peace with China for decades. We travelled across Taiwan to find out if cross-Strait stability could also be a victim of Trumpâ€™s defiance of norms.

items.title: One of Russia's deadliest missile strikes this year hit President Zelenskyyâ€™s hometown | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A deadly Russian missile strike hit Kryvyi Rih, President Zelenskyyâ€™s hometown, killing at least 18 people, including 9 children, and leaving more than 60 injured. The strike also damaged 20 buildings and over 30 vehicles. While Russia claims to have targeted a military gathering, U.S. and European leaders continue pressing for a ceasefire. The following day, Ukrainian drones targeted an industrial facility in Russia's Mordovia, according to the regional governor.

items.title: Who is the most likely buyer at this time?
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has extended the deadline for TikTok to find a buyer, just hours before the current deadline was set to expire. He says the company now has another 75 days to find a new owner. 
The popular video-sharing app could be shut down in the U.S. if it doesn't part ways with its Chinese parent company, ByteDance. Many in Washington believe the app is controlled by the Chinese government, and there are widespread concerns about what it does with the data of its mostly young users.

Chapters:
00:00 Trump extends deadline for TikTok to find buyer
00:42 Dennis-Kenji Kipke, Cyber security expert

items.title: How does this affect disaster relief? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The UN has called for a halt to the fighting in Myanmar to allow the distribution of much needed aid after last week's devastating earthquake. More than 3,300 people have been confirmed dead so far, with hundreds still missing - numbers that analysts fear could still rise dramatically. Myanmar's military and several key armed resistance groups have all declared ceasefires in the wake of the earthquake - but reports are indicating that the junta has continued it's attacks.

Chapters:
00:00 UN calls for end to Myanmar fighting after quake
02:32 Sara Netzer, UNOPS Country director for Myanmar

items.title: Attacks kill at least 18 people in Ukraine, others are injured | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>A Russian missile strike on Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's home city of Krivvy Rih has killed at least 18 people. Officials report nine children are among the dead, and at least 50 people have been injured. Russia's defense ministry claims it was targeting a military gathering, but Ukrainian authorities say the ballistic missile hit a residential area.

items.title: Female senator Akpoti-Uduaghan suspended after harassment claims | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In Nigeria sparked a harassment case involving one of the country's most influential men a growing row over sexism and women's rights. At the heart of the debate is Natasha Akpoti - a Nigerian senator who in a TV interview in late February accused the Senate president, Godswill Akpabio, of making repeated sexual advances towards her. Claims that he has consistently denied.

items.title: Anti-government protests in Turkey: With economic boycotts against Erdogan? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>In an attempt to increase pressure on President Erdogan's government, Turkey's opposition has urged its supporters to boycott dozens of companies with alleged ties to the ruling party. Anti-government protesters also joined calls for a nationwide 'no spending' day.

The boycott campaigns follow the arrest of popular Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu over corruption charges last month, which sparked nationwide protests. Imamoglu has long been considered the strongest candidate to challenge Erdogan in elections scheduled for 2028. While the government has denounced the opposition-led boycott calls as a threat to the economy, many see them as a way to stand up for democratic rights and justice. 

DW Correspondent Julia Hahn reports from Istanbul.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Ukraine ceasefire deal: Is Trump's patience with Putin running out? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a NATO meeting that Trump probably will not accept Russian President Vladimir Putin's delays much longer, dpa news agency reports. The NATO meeting in Brussels was focused on peace in Ukraine, with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte reiterating that Russia is the greatest threat to the alliance. 

Marco Rubio told reporters that Russia's envoy has been in the US to discuss ceasefire plans, and a response from Moscow is expected soon.
